tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship award is significant and requires focused time, but I have a free afternoon and the energy to start immediately.
Starting now creates a buffer for unexpected delays, turning a high-stress deadline into a manageable task without sacrificing future leisure.
Despite 1.5 hours of work, the scholarship application remains largely incomplete with only three days left, confirming my fear that half the free block is already gone.
I made steady progress on the scholarship application, but losing the casual gaming window confirms I must prioritize the deadline over leisure right now.
I've made modest progress on the scholarship application, but with only 1.5 hours of work done in a three-day window and high stress, I realize the remaining time is critically tight.
I've made modest progress on the scholarship application, but with only 1.5 hours of work done in a three-day window and high stress, I realize the remaining time is critically tight.
After 1.5 hours of work, I've reached 69% progress on the scholarship application with high stress and sleep debt, confirming the deadline remains critically tight.
I've reached 73% progress on the scholarship application after one more hour, but my energy is depleted and stress remains high with only three days left.
I slept only two hours, leaving my energy critically low at 2.4 despite having no accumulated sleep debt yet.
I've reached 80% progress on the scholarship application after 1.5 hours of work, but my energy is critically low at 1.5 and stress remains high with only three days left.
After 1.5 hours of work, my scholarship application is now 86% complete with three days remaining, though my stress remains high despite the progress.
The scholarship application is nearly complete at 92% after another hour of work, yet my energy has dropped to zero and stress remains high.

goals.description: Complete the short essay portion of the scholarship application (approx. 60 minutes)
goals.rationale: The application is due in 3 days and requires ~3 hours of focused work; completing the essay first ensures progress on the most time-consuming component before moving to uploads.

goals.rationale: This is a mandatory step for the application completion, which must be done immediately after drafting the essay to finalize the submission within the 3-day deadline.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
